manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: round, time, racket, trunk, ketchup, millionaire, capital, circle, back.
Your opponent's team words are: worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch.
Distractor words are: eagle, olympus, lion.
The kill words are: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe.

message.content:
CLUE: tennis
TARGETS: racket, back, round, time
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue relating to some of our team's words. Must ensure not to clue opponent words or kill words. Also clue cannot be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Words from list: round, time, racket? Actually "racket" is a word but we can clue "tennis". That relates to racket, maybe also "time"? Not really. "Trunk" could relate to "tree". "Ketchup" maybe "tomato". "Millionaire" maybe "rich". "Capital" maybe "city". "Circle" similar to round. "Back" maybe "rear". Need to choose words that are safe. Also avoid linking to opponent words (worm, tooth, torch, board, spy, ground, card, watch). And avoid kill words (suit, paper, post, missile, pipe). Also not use distractors.

We could clue "fruit" which relates to ketchup (tomato is a fruit) and maybe "trunk" (tree trunk). But "fruit" might also relate to "worm" (worms in fruit) which is opponent word, risk. Also maybe "watch" (fruit watch? no). Might be okay but risk.

Better clue "money". Relates to millionaire, capital. Also "back" (back money? maybe back?). Not great.

Clue "circle" but that's a word in list; cannot use. Clue "ring". Relates to round, circle, maybe time (ring of a clock?), back? Not sure. Ring doesn't conflict.

Check opponent words: "watch" could associate with "ring" (ring watch? Not). "board" maybe board game ring? Not likely. "torc" etc.

Kill words: suit, paper, post, missile, pipe. "Ring" not any of those.

Distractors: eagle, olympus, lion. "Ring" no.

So we could clue "ring" relating to round, circle, maybe time (ring of a bell). Also "back" maybe ringback tone? "Racket" ring? not. Could be okay.

But we must list target words we intend. Could be round, circle, time (bell rings), maybe back (ringback). But be careful with association to opponent: "watch" has a ring? watch ring? Not typical. "card" ring? no.

Maybe safer to clue "currency". Relates to millionaire, capital, maybe "back" (back money)? Not good.

Better clue "tree". Relates to trunk, maybe back (back of tree?), round (tree round shape), maybe ketchup (tomato from plant). But could also relate to worm (worms in soil) which is opponent; risk.

Clue "tennis". Relates to racket, maybe time (tennis time? not). Could also relate to back (backhand), round (ball), maybe "capital"? not. But "tennis" not conflict.

We need to be safe: Choose clue "hand". Relates to racket (hand holds racket), backhand, time? no. Round? hand round? Not.

Possibly clue "rich". Relates to millionaire, capital, maybe "back" (backrich? no). Probably safe.

But better to maximize number of targets: "circle" is already a word so not allowed. "round" and "circle" both in list, but clue "orb" maybe relates to round, circle, maybe trunk? no.

Let's pick clue "bank". Relates to capital (bank capital), millionaire (bank), back? not. But "bank" may also relate to "watch"? No.

I think clue "tennis" linking to racket, back (backhand), round (ball is round), maybe time (match time). That is four words. Opponent words: "board" (tennis board? no), "watch" (tennis watch? maybe watch match), but not strong. Kill words: none.

Thus we can give:

CLUE: tennis
TARGETS: racket, back, round, time

Check that "tennis" not part of any lists. Not contain any part like "ten", "nis"? No.

Ok.
